summary: Typed GeoContext flowing through every read command, ambiguity-aware tier decision, structured disambiguation envelope, provider-specific projection methods, radius-based post-filter. Replaces silent wrong-region resolution with an explicit confidence-tier contract the chat agent participates in.
reason: Issue #406 follow-up: prior PRs (#423-#426) fixed named symptoms but real-world Mother's Day testing (2026-05-10) revealed deeper architectural gaps — `_ = flagMetro` discard in restaurants_list.go, hardcoded NYC fallback in availability_check, hardcoded NYC at watch.go:367, and slug-suffix inference that only matched hyphenated input. The redesign makes location a first-class typed concept: ParseLocation parses free-form input (bare city / city+state / coords / metro qualifier), PlaceRegistry holds curated US Places (Bellevue WA/NE/KY, Portland OR/ME, Springfield x4, Columbia x3), confidence.decideTier is ambiguity-aware (specificity + candidate-count + margin drives tier, not absolute popularity), DisambiguationEnvelope is the LOW-tier response, DecorateWithLocationContext attaches location_resolved/location_warning to the response shape (separate from applyGeoFilter which only filters), and ResolveLocation ties it together. Wired into restaurants list / availability check / availability multi-day / earliest / goat / watch with --location and --accept-ambiguous flags. --metro kept as deprecated alias implying --accept-ambiguous (preserves legacy JSON shape contract; never returns envelope). New `location resolve` top-level command exposes the resolver as an MCP primitive. Plan + 2 rounds of multi-persona doc review at docs/plans/2026-05-10-001-feat-location-native-redesign-plan.md.
validated_outcome: Test-first per unit (U1-U9); 11 implementation units total (15 in initial plan, reduced to 11 after round-1 doc-review scope reduction). go build, go vet, go test ./..., govulncheck all green. R14 fixtures F1-F7 pass (bug-report reproductions plus added Portland/Springfield/Columbia ambiguity coverage). NOT YET PUBLISHED to mvanhorn/printing-press-library; user requested local review before PR.
